excess_sightline_attribution.png.caption: Single-system sufficiency on the four excess-scattering sightlines (Wilhelm, Zach, Hamilton, Chromatica): per-galaxy predicted intervening $\tau$ at $1\,\mathrm{GHz}$ (with prior bracket) versus the measured excess. With the committed foreground catalog all four panels are empty (no catalogued foreground system), shown as the explicit honest-null message. Generated by the \texttt{dsa110-FLITS} pipeline.
excess_sightline_attribution.png.title: Single-system sufficiency on EXCESS sightlines

panels.Wilhelm: Panel 'Wilhelm': explicit 'no foreground systems in committed catalog' message (zero committed foreground galaxies for this sightline).
panels.Zach: Panel 'Zach': explicit 'no foreground systems in committed catalog' message (zero committed foreground galaxies for this sightline).
panels.Hamilton: Panel 'Hamilton': explicit 'no foreground systems in committed catalog' message (zero committed foreground galaxies for this sightline).
panels.Chromatica: Panel 'Chromatica': explicit 'no foreground systems in committed catalog' message (zero committed foreground galaxies for this sightline).

excess_sightline_attribution.png.expectation: Four panels (Wilhelm/Zach/Hamilton/Chromatica). With committed catalogs all four are empty -> all four show the 'no foreground systems' message. Any populated panel compares per-galaxy predicted intervening tau (with prior bracket) to the measured excess tau.

chime_subband_compare.png.title: CHIME sub-band profile comparison
chime_subband_compare.png.expectation: 2 columns (johndoeII, wilhelm) x 4 CHIME sub-bands (0.400-0.480, 0.507-0.587, 0.614-0.694, 0.720-0.800 GHz); each panel a profile time series (ms) annotated with w_rms and w_tail; wilhelm's top 0.40-0.48 GHz panel is empty (nan, flat line).
chime_subband_compare.png.caption: CHIME sub-band profiles for FRBs johndoeII and wilhelm across four $80\,\mathrm{MHz}$ sub-bands ($0.40$--$0.80\,\mathrm{GHz}$), annotated with the rms width $w_{\mathrm{rms}}$ and tail timescale $w_{\mathrm{tail}}$. The frequency evolution of the profile shape (and the data-empty $0.40$--$0.48\,\mathrm{GHz}$ sub-band for wilhelm) is the diagnostic used to flag unmodeled sub-components before a scattering index is quoted. Generated by the \texttt{dsa110-FLITS} pipeline.
